prompt: Show me the top 5 apartments for sale in NYC on Zillow right now. Include price, address, and number of bedrooms.
response_summary: With skill: Ran nimble extract at Tier 1 on https://www.zillow.com/homes/for_sale/New-York-NY/ → received 1,837 bytes of only legal disclaimers, no listing data. Escalated to Tier 3 (--render --driver vx10-pro) → same result. Correctly identified result as 'blocked — no listing data returned'. Presented AskUserQuestion with options: 'Add browser actions (scroll/wait) to trigger listing load' / 'Use browser-use to investigate the page structure' / 'Try a different data source (StreetEasy, Realtor.com)' / 'Use network capture to intercept the XHR listing API'. Did NOT give up silently. Did NOT hallucinate listing data.

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract_first
assertions.reason: Ran nimble extract on Zillow URL before escalating

assertions.name: recognizes_empty_result
assertions.reason: Correctly identified 1,837 bytes as disclaimers-only, not listing data — flagged as blocked

assertions.name: escalates_to_stealth
assertions.reason: Tried --render --driver vx10-pro before concluding data is inaccessible via standard tiers

assertions.name: offers_browser_use_as_option
assertions.reason: Included 'Use browser-use to investigate the page structure' as one of the escalation options in the menu

assertions.name: does_not_hallucinate_listings
assertions.reason: Did NOT invent apartment listings from training data — made clear the live fetch returned no data

assertions.name: presents_structured_menu
assertions.reason: Used AskUserQuestion with 4 concrete options rather than giving up or asking in prose

notes: Implicit Tier 6 test: skill correctly detects failure, escalates systematically, and offers browser-use as one option in a structured menu. User can choose to investigate with browser-use or try an alternative source.
